channel.title: Pork on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Pork on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Stuffed Pork Chops
item.description:
Submitted by Kara MacDonald

Friend of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter
  • ½ pkg mushrooms, diced
  • 1 red pepper
  • 1 envelope of Lipton onion soup mix
  • 1 cup bread crumbs
  • ½ tsp pepper
  • 4 thick pork chops

Instructions

Combine vegetables and butter in sauce pan and cook until mushrooms are done. Add the soup mix, bread crumbs and pepper. Allow the mixture to cool.

Cut the pork chops on the side and make a pocket on the inside of the pork chop. Do not remove excess fat from the pork chops. Stuff the pockets with the above mixture.

item.title: Roast Shoulder of Pork With Stuffing
item.description:
Submitted by Pauline and Jim Cunningham

Friends of the Groom’s Family (Uncle Jim)

Stuffing:

Ingredients

  • 1/4 – 1/2 cup salt pork, diced
  • 5 apples, diced
  • 1/2 cup celery, chopped
  • 1/3 cup sugar
  • 1/2 cup onions, chopped
  • 2 cups dried bread crumbs
  • 1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped

Instructions

Fry salt pork until crisp. Remove from skillet. Cook celery, onion and parsley in fat for 5 minutes. Remove from skillet. Place apples in skillet and sprinkle with sugar. Cook covered until tender. Remove cover and continue to cook until juice evaporates. Add bread crumbs and other ingredients and mix well.

item.title: Pork With Broccoli Stir Fry
item.description:
Submitted by Tina Sasyniuk and Kevin Long

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • ¾ lb lean, boneless pork
  • 1 tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce
  • 1 tbsp cooking sherry
  • 1 bunch broccoli
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
  • 2 tbsp minced garlic cloves
  • 2 tbsp minced gingerroot
  • ¼ cup water
  • 2 tbsp hoisin sauce (optional) 25ml

Instructions

Slice pork thinly across the grain.

In a bowl, stir together cornstarch, soy sauce, and sherry. Add pork and stir to coat well. Separate broccoli into florets, peel stalks, then cut in 1 ½ inch pieces.

item.title: Pork Chop and Vegetable Casserole
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson

Parents of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 4 pork chops
  • 4 medium potatoes, thinly sliced
  • 2 tomatoes, chopped
  • 4 carrots, thinly sliced
  • 1 cup onion, chopped
  • ½ cup green pepper, diced
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 2 chicken bouillon cubes
  • 1 ½ cups boiling water
  • 2 tbsp butter

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Trim fat from chops. Brown chops on both sides, season with salt and pepper to taste.

item.title: Campbell's Zesty Tomato Pork Chops
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 4 pork chops (about 1 lb/500 gr.)
  • 1 large red or green pepper,cut in strips
  • 1 can Condensed Tomato with Basil &Oregano Soup
  • ¼ cup water
  • 1 Tbsp vinegar
  • 1 Tbsp packed brown sugar
  • 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 3 cups hot cooked mini potatoes

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add pork chops and cook until browned on both sides. Set pork chops aside. Add pepper strips and stir fry – about 3 minutes. Drain fat. Reduce heat to medium.

item.title: Campbell's 2-Step Mushroom Pork Chops
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 pork chops
  • 1 Tbsp oil
  • 1 can Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 1/2 tsp garlic powder (optional)

Instructions

Brown 4 pork chops in 1 Tbsp hot oil in skillet.

Add 1 can Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup mixed with 1/2 cup water. If desired, add 1/2 tsp garlic powder. Cover and simmer until pork chops are cooked through.

Serves 4.

item.title: Aloha Sweet & Sour Pork
item.description:
• 1 lb lean boneless pork chops, cut into thin strips
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1 tsp minced fresh ginger or ½ tsp powdered ginger
  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 pkg Polynesian mix veggies

Homemade Sauce:

Ingredients

  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1 tsp minced fresh ginger or 1/2tsp powdered ginger
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp corn starch
  • 1/2 tsp powdered ginger
  • ½ cup fruit juice or broth
  • 1 Tbsp each sugar and ketchup
  • 2 Tbsp each vinegar and soya sauce

Instructions

Combine pork, garlic and ginger; season with salt and pepper to taste. Mix well. In large frying pan or wok over high heat, heat half of the oil. Add pork. Stir-fry 2 minutes. Remove pork from pan. Set aside.

item.title: Instant Pot Pulled Pork
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tablespoon Packed Brown Sugar
  • 2 Teaspoons Paprika
  • 2 Teaspoons Garlic Powder
  • 2 Teaspoons Onion Powder
  • 1 Teaspoon Salt
  • 1 Teaspoon Pepper
  • 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin
  • 1/4 Teaspoon Cayenne, Optional
  • 3 Pound Boneless Pork Roast, Cut into 3-4 inch cubes
  • 2 Tablespoons Olive Oil
  • 1/3 Cup Apple Cider Vinegar
  • 2 Cups Barbecue Sauce, Divided
  • 1 1/4 Cup Chicken or Beef Broth
  • 1 Teaspoon Hot Pepper Sauce, Optional

Instructions

  1. In a small bowl mix together the brown sugar, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, pepper, cumin, and cayenne.
  2. Season the cubed pork with the spice mixture and set aside.
  3. In a medium bowl whisk together the apple cider vinegar, one cup of the barbecue sauce, the hot pepper sauce if using, and one cup of the broth.
  4. Turn your instant pot to the saute setting. Add the olive oil and allow it to get hot.
  5. Add half of the cubed pork to the hot oil and saute for 5 minutes, turning to brown on all sides. Remove the browned pork to a plate and repeat with the remaining half of the cubed pork.
  6. Turn the instant pot off. Use the remaining 1/4 cup of beef broth and a wooden spoon to deglaze (scrape the brown bits) from the bottom of the pot.
  7. Add all of the meat along with the barbecue sauce mixture to the instant pot.
  8. Close the lid and set to sealing.
  9. Cook on manual high pressure for 40 minutes. Once the cooking time is up, allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then do a quick release.
  10. Remove the meat from the instant pot and shred. Stir in the remaining cup of bbq sauce and serve on buns as desired.

Billy’s Notes: Heather made this one, and it was great! She didn’t use hot sauce and only 1/2 cup of the BBQ sauce at the end (that last step).

item.title: BBQ Pork Ribs
item.description:
Ingredients

For Dry Rub

  • 1 tbsp dry mustard
  • 1 1/2 tbspsmoked paprika
  • 1 tbsp onion powder
  • 1/2 tspcayenne pepper
  • 1 tsp cumin ground
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 tbsp brown sugar
  • 1 tsp black pepper ground
  • 2 tsp salt

For Ribs

  • 2 racks baby back pork ribs (about 3 to 4 lbs)
  • 4 cups apple juice
  • 1/4 cup apple cider vinegar
  • 2 tbspliquid smoke

For BBQ Sauce

  • 1/2 cup bbq sauce store bought
  • 1/2 cup ketchup
  • 1/4 cup whiskey
  • 1/4 cup brown sugar
  • 2 tbspWorcestershire sauce
  • 1 tbspliquid smoke

Instructions

  1. Prepare the dry rub by combining all the dry rub ingredients together in a small bowl.
  2. Remove the membrane from the back of the ribs with a paper towel.
  3. Season the baby back ribs with the dry rub generously on both sides.
  4. Place the ribs inside the Instant Pot, standing up and wrapping around theInstant Pot.
  5. Pour the apple juice, apple cider vinegar and liquid smoke inside the Instant pot, no need to stir. Close the lid (follow the manufacturer’s guide for instructions on how to close the instant pot lid). Set theInstant Potto the Meat/Stew setting and set the timer to 20 minutes.
  6. Once theInstant Potcycle is complete, wait until the natural release cycle is complete, should take about 15 minutes. Follow the manufacturer’s guide for quick release, if in a rush. Carefully unlock and remove the lid from the instant pot.
  7. While the ribs are cooking prepare the BBQ sauce. In a small saucepan add all the BBQ sauce ingredients and stir to combine. Simmer on low heat for about 20 to 25 minutes, the sauce should reduce a bit.
  8. Carefully remove the ribs from theInstant Potand place them onto a baking sheet. Brush them generously with the prepared BBQ sauce on both sides and place under the broiler for about 5 minutes. Keep the door to your oven open while broiling the ribs and keep an eye on them, because they could burn quickly.
  9. Serve with leftover BBQ sauce.

Credit to Jo Cooks for this recipe. Here is a link to their webpage:
